title: Java Lambda Functional
description: Practice a Comparator lambda and list sorting.

completionChecks.prompt: What does names.sort(Comparator.comparing(String::length)) do to the order?
choices: Sorts shortest strings first, ascending by length
completionChecks.explanation: Comparator.comparing(String::length) orders by the int length in natural ascending order, so shorter strings sort before longer ones.

completionChecks.prompt: What ordering does compareToIgnoreCase provide in the first lambda?
choices: Alphabetical ordering that ignores letter case
A different random order each run
completionChecks.explanation: compareToIgnoreCase compares text lexicographically without regard to uppercase versus lowercase, giving case-insensitive alphabetical order.

completionChecks.prompt: What does names contain after sorting ["Bob", "Al", "Cara"] with Comparator.comparing(String::length)?
completionChecks.explanation: The method reference extracts each String length, so the comparator orders the names from shortest to longest.
